Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Richard L. Daniels, Esq.
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS BRIAN UGALE, STEVEN LAUER, AND BRENT LANGLOIS' Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' unopposed motion to compel and for sanctions is granted. The moving papers show that Plaintiffs served on defendant George Goodwin Realty (GGR) a set of form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production of documents. GGR has not responded. The motion to compel is granted and objections are waived. GGR is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, to this discovery within 20 days of entry of this order. (See CCP 2031.300(a); 2030.290(a).) GGR is ordered to produce responsive documents within 20 days of entry of this order.

Plaintiffs also seek sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) and 2030.290(c) provide that the court shall order sanctions payable to a party who successfully obtains a motion to compel unless the opposing party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust. The court does not find substantial justification by GGR and orders sanctions in the amount of $2000 payable by GGR to Plaintiffs within 20 days of entry of this order. The court reduces the requested sanctions because Plaintiffs do not provide a declaration substantiating the amount of fees and the amount awarded is reasonable on a straightforward motion like this one.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(301/CVA)
